channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Bihar Women's Panel Issues Notice Over Pappu Yadav's Remarks on Women's Participation in Politics
item.description: Independent MP Pappu Yadav's controversial remarks about the political and social status of women in India have drawn sharp criticism and attention. Speaking about the recently defeated women’s reserv...

item.title: DP Kithure Kindiki Urges Ameru People to Back President Ruto for Second Term During Meru Tour
item.description: Deputy President Kithure Kindiki has clearly told the Ameru community in Meru County that they have no option but to support President William Ruto in the 2027 General Election. While speaking during ...

item.title: DENR Alerts Public on Spread of Invasive Finlayson’s Squirrels in Metro Manila
item.description: The Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR) recently issued a public advisory about the increasing sightings of non-native Finlayson’s squirrels in Metro Manila and nearby provinces. Na...

item.title: Nawaz Sharif Appreciates Pakistan’s Role in US-Iran Mediation and Commends Punjab Governance
item.description: Three-time former Prime Minister and PML-N President Nawaz Sharif has publicly praised the federal government and Chief of Army Staff, Field Marshal Asim Munir, for their efforts in mediating between ...

item.title: Setlhodi reflects on enduring legacy of the Soweto Derby
item.description: The iconic rivalry between Orlando Pirates and Kaizer Chiefs, known as the Soweto Derby, remains a central part of South African football culture. According to legendary Kaizer Chiefs goalkeeper Josep...

item.title: Mark Carney Forms Advisory Council on Canada-U.S. Relations with Former Political Leaders
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has established a new council to provide strategic advice on Canada-U.S. relations, including economic and trade matters. The council features notable figures from both poli...

item.title: South Korean Police Pursue Arrest of Hybe Chairman Bang Si-Hyuk Over Alleged Investor Fraud
item.description: South Korean police have requested an arrest warrant for Bang Si-Hyuk, the billionaire founder and chairman of Hybe, the entertainment company behind global K-pop sensation BTS. Authorities allege tha...

item.title: Michael Jackson biopic criticised for poor execution and lack of depth
item.description: The Michael Jackson biopic, directed by Antoine Fuqua and starring his nephew Jaafar Jackson, has been widely panned as a tedious and poorly executed film. Spanning Jackson’s life from 1966 to 1988, t...

item.title: Creamline takes commanding Game 1 win over Cignal in PVL All-Filipino Finals
item.description: Malakas ang naging simula ng Creamline Cool Smashers sa PVL All-Filipino Finals matapos nilang dominahin ang Cignal Super Spikers sa Game 1 sa pamamagitan ng isang straight-sets victory. Ipinakita ng ...

item.title: Embassy source says Zaldy Co not listed in Portuguese citizens registry amid passport questions
item.description: A source from the Portuguese Embassy in Prague said that former Ako Bicol party-list Representative Zaldy Co does not appear in their official citizens’ registry, contradicting earlier suspicions that...

item.title: Union Bank Marks 109 Years of Serving Nigerians and Building the Economy
item.description: Union Bank of Nigeria dey celebrate im 109th anniversary on April 21, 2026. Di bank wey start as Colonial Bank for 1917 don stand strong through plenty generations, even before Nigeria become independ...

item.title: Sowore Files No-Case Submission in DSS Defamation Lawsuit
item.description: Human rights activist Omoloye Sowore has taken a significant step in his legal battle with the Department of State Services (DSS) by filing a no-case submission at the Federal High Court in Abuja. The...

item.title: Pakistan Navy tests indigenous ‘Taimoor’ air-launched anti-ship cruise missile, enhancing maritime strike capability
item.description: The Pakistan Navy has successfully carried out a live test of its indigenously developed air-launched cruise missile, named ‘Taimoor’, according to a statement issued by the Inter-Services Public Rela...

item.title: The Impact of Losing a Key Bus Route on a Cornwall Village
item.description: In Mousehole, Cornwall, the loss of a crucial bus route has left residents, especially the elderly, struggling to access essential services. The village, once known for its butcher, post office, and g...

item.title: Sir Olly Robbins reveals No 10 lobbied for Starmer's Director of Communications to receive diplomatic posting
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer's officials secretly pushed for his Director of Communications, Matthew Doyle, to be given a senior diplomatic role abroad, according to evidence given by the sacked senior mandarin S...

item.title: Amazon Expands Anthropic Partnership with Up to $25 Billion Investment and Long-Term AI Infrastructure Commitment
item.description: Amazon is significantly deepening its partnership with artificial intelligence company Anthropic through a new agreement that could see the tech giant invest up to $25 billion more into the startup. T...

item.title: Planting diverse forests, not monoculture plantations, is key to restoring biodiversity and climate resilience
item.description: Around the world, governments and organizations are planning to plant more than 1 trillion trees this decade to slow climate change and reduce biodiversity loss. Yet, many of these efforts rely on mon...

item.title: Nigeria and South Africa expand naval cooperation to tackle piracy and maritime crime
item.description: Nigeria and South Africa have taken steps to strengthen their naval partnership, focusing on improving maritime security across the African continent. This follows their first-ever Naval Staff Talks h...

item.title: Canada Participates in UN Committee Vote for Iran Despite Denials
item.description: On April 8, 2026, during a United Nations Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC) meeting, Canada supported the appointment of Iran to the UN Committee for Program and Coordination, which oversees variou...

item.title: President Ruto Warns Diaspora Kenyans About Social Media Misinformation
item.description: President William Ruto, during a meeting with Kenyans in Rome, Italy, cautioned them against relying on social media for news about Kenya. He emphasized that social media platforms are often flooded w...
